question:
For a positive integer $n$, define $d(n)$ to be the sum of the digits of $n$ when written in binary (for example, $d(13) = 1+1+0+1=3)$. Let
\[
S = \sum_{k=1}^{2020} (-1)^{d(k)} k^3.
\]
Determine $S$ modulo 2020.
solution:
Note that
$$
(1-x)(1-x^2)(1-x^4)\cdots(1-x^{1024})=\sum_{k=0}^{2047}(-1)^{d(k)}x^k
$$
and
$$
x^{2016}(1-x)(1-x^2)\cdots(1-x^{16})=\sum_{k=2016}^{2047}(-1)^{d(k)}x^k.
$$
Applying $x\frac{d}{dx}$ to both sides of each of these two equations three times, and then setting $x=1$, shows that
$$
\sum_{k=0}^{2047}(-1)^{d(k)}k^3 = \sum_{k=2016}^{2047}(-1)^{d(k)}k^3 = 0,
$$
and therefore
$$
\sum_{k=1}^{2015}(-1)^{d(k)}k^3 = 0.
$$
Hence we may write
\begin{align*}
S &=\sum_{k=2016}^{2020}(-1)^{d(k)}k^3 \\
 &= \sum_{k=0}^4 (-1)^{d(k)} (k+2016)^3 \\
&\equiv (-4)^3 + (-1)(-3)^3+(-1)(-2)^3+(1)(-1)^3 \\
&= -64+27+8-1 \\
&\equiv -30\equiv 1990\pmod{2020}.
\end{align*}

\noindent
\textbf{Remark.} The function $d(n)$ appears in the OEIS as sequence A000120.

descriptive_long.question:
For a positive integer integer, define digisum(integer) to be the sum of the digits of integer when written in binary (for example, digisum(13) = 1+1+0+1=3). Let
\[
summation = \sum_{indexer=1}^{2020} (-1)^{digisum(indexer)} indexer^3.
\]
Determine summation modulo 2020.
descriptive_long.solution:
Note that
$$
(1-variable)(1-variable^2)(1-variable^4)\cdots(1-variable^{1024})=\sum_{indexer=0}^{2047}(-1)^{digisum(indexer)}variable^{indexer}
$$
and
$$
variable^{2016}(1-variable)(1-variable^2)\cdots(1-variable^{16})=\sum_{indexer=2016}^{2047}(-1)^{digisum(indexer)}variable^{indexer}.
$$
Applying $variable\frac{digisum}{digisum variable}$ to both sides of each of these two equations three times, and then setting $variable=1$, shows that
$$
\sum_{indexer=0}^{2047}(-1)^{digisum(indexer)}indexer^3 = \sum_{indexer=2016}^{2047}(-1)^{digisum(indexer)}indexer^3 = 0,
$$
and therefore
$$
\sum_{indexer=1}^{2015}(-1)^{digisum(indexer)}indexer^3 = 0.
$$
Hence we may write
\begin{align*}
summation &=\sum_{indexer=2016}^{2020}(-1)^{digisum(indexer)}indexer^3 \
 &= \sum_{indexer=0}^4 (-1)^{digisum(indexer)} (indexer+2016)^3 \\
&\equiv (-4)^3 + (-1)(-3)^3+(-1)(-2)^3+(1)(-1)^3 \\
&= -64+27+8-1 \\
&\equiv -30\equiv 1990\pmod{2020}.
\end{align*}

\noindent
\textbf{Remark.} The function digisum(integer) appears in the OEIS as sequence A000120.

descriptive_long_confusing.question:
For a positive integer $pebblestone$, define $\meadowlark(pebblestone)$ to be the sum of the digits of $pebblestone$ when written in binary (for example, $\meadowlark(13) = 1+1+0+1=3)$. Let
\[
rainshadow = \sum_{rivertrail=1}^{2020} (-1)^{\meadowlark(rivertrail)} rivertrail^3.
\]
Determine $rainshadow$ modulo 2020.
descriptive_long_confusing.solution:
Note that
$$
(1-\lanternfly)(1-\lanternfly^2)(1-\lanternfly^4)\cdots(1-\lanternfly^{1024})=\sum_{rivertrail=0}^{2047}(-1)^{\meadowlark(rivertrail)}\lanternfly^{rivertrail}
$$
and
$$
\lanternfly^{2016}(1-\lanternfly)(1-\lanternfly^2)\cdots(1-\lanternfly^{16})=\sum_{rivertrail=2016}^{2047}(-1)^{\meadowlark(rivertrail)}\lanternfly^{rivertrail}.
$$
Applying $\lanternfly\frac{d}{d\lanternfly}$ to both sides of each of these two equations three times, and then setting $\lanternfly=1$, shows that
$$
\sum_{rivertrail=0}^{2047}(-1)^{\meadowlark(rivertrail)}rivertrail^3 = \sum_{rivertrail=2016}^{2047}(-1)^{\meadowlark(rivertrail)}rivertrail^3 = 0,
$$
and therefore
$$
\sum_{rivertrail=1}^{2015}(-1)^{\meadowlark(rivertrail)}rivertrail^3 = 0.
$$
Hence we may write
\begin{align*}
rainshadow &= \sum_{rivertrail=2016}^{2020} (-1)^{\meadowlark(rivertrail)} rivertrail^3 \\ & = \sum_{rivertrail=0}^4 (-1)^{\meadowlark(rivertrail)} (rivertrail+2016)^3 \\ &\equiv (-4)^3 + (-1)(-3)^3 + (-1)(-2)^3 + (1)(-1)^3 \\ &= -64 + 27 + 8 - 1 \\ &\equiv -30 \equiv 1990 \pmod{2020}.
\end{align*}

\noindent
\textbf{Remark.} The function $\meadowlark(pebblestone)$ appears in the OEIS as sequence A000120.

descriptive_long_misleading.question:
For a positive integer $fractional$, define $productdigits(fractional)$ to be the sum of the digits of $fractional$ when written in binary (for example, $productdigits(13) = 1+1+0+1=3)$. Let
\[
differencevalue = \sum_{continuum=1}^{2020} (-1)^{productdigits(continuum)} continuum^3.
\]
Determine $differencevalue$ modulo 2020.
descriptive_long_misleading.solution:
Note that
$$
(1-constantc)(1-constantc^2)(1-constantc^4)\cdots(1-constantc^{1024})=\sum_{continuum=0}^{2047}(-1)^{productdigits(continuum)}constantc^{continuum}
$$
and
$$
constantc^{2016}(1-constantc)(1-constantc^2)\cdots(1-constantc^{16})=\sum_{continuum=2016}^{2047}(-1)^{productdigits(continuum)}constantc^{continuum}.
$$
Applying $constantc\frac{productdigits}{productdigits\,constantc}$ to both sides of each of these two equations three times, and then setting $constantc=1$, shows that
$$
\sum_{continuum=0}^{2047}(-1)^{productdigits(continuum)}continuum^3 = \sum_{continuum=2016}^{2047}(-1)^{productdigits(continuum)}continuum^3 = 0,
$$
and therefore
$$
\sum_{continuum=1}^{2015}(-1)^{productdigits(continuum)}continuum^3 = 0.
$$
Hence we may write
\begin{align*}
differencevalue &=\sum_{continuum=2016}^{2020}(-1)^{productdigits(continuum)}continuum^3 \
 &= \sum_{continuum=0}^4 (-1)^{productdigits(continuum)} (continuum+2016)^3 \\
&\equiv (-4)^3 + (-1)(-3)^3+(-1)(-2)^3+(1)(-1)^3 \\
&= -64+27+8-1 \\
&\equiv -30\equiv 1990\pmod{2020}.
\end{align*}

\noindent
\textbf{Remark.} The function $productdigits(fractional)$ appears in the OEIS as sequence A000120.

garbled_string.question:
For a positive integer $qzxwvtnp$, define $zplskqnv(qzxwvtnp)$ to be the sum of the digits of $qzxwvtnp$ when written in binary (for example, $zplskqnv(13) = 1+1+0+1=3)$. Let
\[
fwhztdrm = \sum_{hjgrksla=1}^{2020} (-1)^{zplskqnv(hjgrksla)} hjgrksla^3.
\]
Determine $fwhztdrm$ modulo 2020.
garbled_string.solution:
Note that
$$
(1-bmnclrtu)(1-bmnclrtu^2)(1-bmnclrtu^4)\cdots(1-bmnclrtu^{1024})=\sum_{hjgrksla=0}^{2047}(-1)^{zplskqnv(hjgrksla)}bmnclrtu^{hjgrksla}
$$
and
$$
bmnclrtu^{2016}(1-bmnclrtu)(1-bmnclrtu^2)\cdots(1-bmnclrtu^{16})=\sum_{hjgrksla=2016}^{2047}(-1)^{zplskqnv(hjgrksla)}bmnclrtu^{hjgrksla}.
$$
Applying $bmnclrtu\frac{d}{d bmnclrtu}$ to both sides of each of these two equations three times, and then setting $bmnclrtu=1$, shows that
$$
\sum_{hjgrksla=0}^{2047}(-1)^{zplskqnv(hjgrksla)}hjgrksla^3 = \sum_{hjgrksla=2016}^{2047}(-1)^{zplskqnv(hjgrksla)}hjgrksla^3 = 0,
$$
and therefore
$$
\sum_{hjgrksla=1}^{2015}(-1)^{zplskqnv(hjgrksla)}hjgrksla^3 = 0.
$$
Hence we may write
\begin{align*}
fwhztdrm &=\sum_{hjgrksla=2016}^{2020}(-1)^{zplskqnv(hjgrksla)}hjgrksla^3 \\
 &= \sum_{hjgrksla=0}^4 (-1)^{zplskqnv(hjgrksla)} (hjgrksla+2016)^3 \\
&\equiv (-4)^3 + (-1)(-3)^3+(-1)(-2)^3+(1)(-1)^3 \\
&= -64+27+8-1 \\
&\equiv -30\equiv 1990\pmod{2020}.
\end{align*}

\noindent
\textbf{Remark.} The function $zplskqnv(qzxwvtnp)$ appears in the OEIS as sequence A000120.

core_steps: Encode (−1)^{d(k)} as coefficients of the generating function ∏_{i≥0}(1−x^{2^{i}}).
Pick a power-of-two length 2^t exceeding the summation limit and show, via taking ‘power’ many derivatives at x=1, that the k^power-weighted sum over the full block [0,2^t−1] is 0.
Use a suitably shifted and truncated version of the product to prove a second 0-sum over the block that starts a little before the limit, so that only a short tail remains.
Express the desired S as the sum over that small tail and evaluate it term-by-term.
Reduce the numerical tail to obtain the answer modulo the given modulus.

N.description: Upper limit of the original sum (and modulus used at the end).  Must be smaller than 2^t but otherwise arbitrary.

power.description: Exponent on k in the summand; determines how many derivatives are taken.

big_power_of_two.description: Largest power of two used in the full generating-function product; 2^t with 2^t > N.

max_index.description: Greatest index in the full block, equal to 2·big_power_of_two − 1.

small_power_of_two.description: Largest power of two kept in the truncated product used for the shifted block; choose so that (#factors) > power.

shift_start.description: First index of the residual tail; chosen so that [shift_start, max_index] is the zero-sum block coming from the shifted product.

tail_count.description: Number of terms actually computed by hand, N − shift_start + 1.
